story_paragraphs: A direct homepage for restaurants that need clear dishes, clear prices, and fast action.
Use this preset for local family dining, takeaway-first operators, and neighborhood value concepts.

story_paragraphs: Maison Elise ist eine moderne Brasserie fur Gaste, die mittags leicht, abends stilvoll und zu besonderen Anlassen mit Ruhe genießen wollen.
Zwischen Lunch, Apero und Dinner entsteht ein Haus mit saisonaler Karte, warmer Servicehaltung und einer klaren urbanen Eleganz.

homeSignatures.title: Saisonale Signaturen fur Lunch und Dinner
homeSignatures.body: Ein kuratierter Einstieg in die Handschrift der Kuche: französische Brasserie-Klassiker, leichter interpretiert und fur einen urbanen Tagesrhythmus komponiert.

menu.title: Unsere Menus fur Mittag, Abend und den kleinen Anlass dazwischen
menu.body: Die Karte bleibt bewusst fokussiert: saisonale Gerichte, klare Handschrift, starke Wiedererkennbarkeit und genug Raum fur Specials, Pairings und Anlassdinner.

reservation.title: Tischanfragen fur Lunch, Dinner und besondere Abende
reservation.body: Vom schnellen Business Lunch bis zum gesetzten Dinner: die digitale Reservierung soll so ruhig und hochwertig wirken wie der eigentliche Empfang im Restaurant.

contact.title: Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Nachricht
contact.body: Fragen zu Reservierungen, Private Dining, Events oder Presseanfragen lassen sich ohne Layoutwechsel direkt im selben Premium-Auftritt abbilden.

journal_cards.title: Wir feiern 10 Jahre Brasserie-Kultur

journal_cards.title: Die französische Kuche: der Gipfel der Kochkunst

theme-luxury-b.restaurant_tagline: A cinematic Berlin dining room shaped by shadow, copper, and appetite.
theme-luxury-b.hero_subheadline: No visible navigation, only a strong house signature: handwritten branding, a monumental monogram, warm plates in dark light, and a dining room that feels remembered before it is fully explained.
theme-luxury-b.story_body: Maison Verenne should feel like an evening invitation rather than a practical interface. The guest sees atmosphere first, then cuisine, then the small number of actions that matter.
story_paragraphs: The room is intentionally quiet in its interface language: no visible tabs leading the eye away, only atmosphere, appetite, and a few deliberate pathways into menu, reservation, and contact.
Copper accents and handwritten gestures soften the dark structure, while food, glassware, and faces hold the light. That contrast is what makes the homepage sell emotionally.
theme-luxury-b.story_heading: A house that leads with mood, then lets food and ritual confirm it
theme-luxury-b.feature_title: Dark composition, warm dishes, and a room staged like a memory
theme-luxury-b.feature_body: The visual hierarchy stays restrained: oversized monogram, thin copper ornament, white typography, and imagery placed asymmetrically so the page feels composed rather than templated.
theme-luxury-b.reserve_heading: Reserve for dinner, late cocktails, or a longer table worth dressing for
theme-luxury-b.reserve_body: The booking area should feel soft and elegant, with enough friction removed that it converts easily while still keeping the tone of a premium evening house.
theme-luxury-b.closing_heading: A closing note in white type, copper detail, and the promise of another evening
theme-luxury-b.closing_body: Address, opening hours, and reservation paths sit quietly at the end. The brand should linger more than the interface does.

homeSignatures.title: Three entry points into the same evening
homeSignatures.body: Menu presentation should feel more like elegant invitation cards than content blocks. Each lane is a promise: aperitif, dinner, and after-dinner rhythm.

menu.title: Minimal, icon-led pathways into the house offer
menu.body: Keep the first encounter curated and light. Show only the essence, not the entire information architecture.

menuCategories.title: A homepage built from appetite, atmosphere, and reservation intent

reservation.title: A gentle conversion point inside a strong emotional composition
reservation.body: The user should feel invited, not processed. Keep the booking prompt near imagery so it feels part of the experience rather than an isolated form step.

contact.title: Address, hours, and direct contact placed quietly at the end
contact.body: Like Belmondo, practical information should be visible but never louder than the atmosphere itself.

marketFit.title: A small editorial layer to deepen the emotional tone

location_cards.label: Dining room
location_cards.title: A dark room where plates and glassware hold the light
location_cards.body: The architecture is not explained too early. It is felt through shadow, warm tabletops, and an intimate copper line that frames the page like ornament rather than UI.

location_cards.label: Cocktail corner
location_cards.title: A second rhythm for guests who begin at the bar
location_cards.body: The site should support the feeling that one can arrive for a drink and remain for dinner, dessert, and a final round without crossing into a different brand language.

location_cards.label: Private table
location_cards.title: A more intimate frame for hosted evenings
location_cards.body: For discreet celebrations, private dinners, and occasions where the tone must remain elegant, controlled, and emotionally rich.

journal_cards.title: Food should glow out of the darkness
journal_cards.body: The page composition works when the background withdraws and the warm tones of the dish, candle, and glass become the visual memory.

journal_cards.title: The homepage is the sales moment
journal_cards.body: Not a navigation hub, but the main emotional proof that the place is worth visiting. The brand promise has to land before the user starts comparing options.

journal_cards.title: Copper detail makes the darkness feel crafted
journal_cards.body: Thin ornamental lines and a restrained metallic accent add softness and signature without turning the page into a decorative cliché.

menu_link_cards.label: Aperitif
menu_link_cards.title: Cocktails & oysters
menu_link_cards.body: A lighter entry into the evening, designed for guests who arrive for one glass and stay for more.

menu_link_cards.label: Dinner
menu_link_cards.title: Cuisine & signature plates
menu_link_cards.body: Main dishes and warmer compositions that give the site its appetite and seriousness.

menu_link_cards.label: After Dinner
menu_link_cards.title: Dessert & late pours
menu_link_cards.body: A slower final chapter with darker sweets, polished cocktails, and a more intimate tone.

items.name: Mousse au Chocolat

category_cards.label: Mood
category_cards.title: An emotional homepage first
category_cards.body: Navigation recedes so the brand and atmosphere can carry the first impression.

category_cards.label: Cuisine
category_cards.title: Food as the warm focal point
category_cards.body: Dishes, drinks, and table light do more persuasive work than interface chrome.

category_cards.label: Reserve
category_cards.title: Small number of clear actions
category_cards.body: Menu, reservation, and contact appear deliberately, never as a crowded set of options.

market_fit.label: Positioning
market_fit.title: Belmondo sells feeling before information
market_fit.body: This variant should work best for brands that need to create desire quickly through atmosphere and visual signature.

market_fit.label: Conversion
market_fit.title: Reservation sits inside the composition
market_fit.body: The booking moment is stronger when it appears near imagery and brand emotion, not as a separate transactional block.

market_fit.label: Design
market_fit.title: Copper detail, white type, dark stage
market_fit.body: The palette should remain restrained so the imagery and monogram feel expensive rather than loud.

market_fit.label: Behavior
market_fit.title: Minimal navigation keeps the eye on the homepage
market_fit.body: The concept is meant for places where the homepage itself does most of the persuasion work.

signature_items.name: Canard Roti
signature_items.description: Cherry jus, glazed shallots, pommes fondantes.
